archimate:ApplicationInterface.name: eProcurement
archimate:ApplicationInterface.documentation:
Definition: eProcurement as a web service is a method of communication and data exchange between different software applications or systems involved in the eProcurement process. It enables interoperability and integration between various eProcurement platforms, systems, or other software solutions. By using standard web protocols, such as HTTP, it allows different components of an eProcurement ecosystem to interact and share information seamlessly. This facilitates efficient and streamlined procurement processes, enabling organizations to connect and exchange data with suppliers, manage procurement workflows, and optimize their procurement activities.
Source: European Commission
Source reference: https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/single-market/public-procurement/digital-procurement_en
LOST view: TVA-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/EProcurementApplicationInterface
ABB name: egovera:EProcurementApplicationInterface
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/EProcurementApplicationInterface
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/WebServiceApplicationInterface

properties.value: eProcurement as a web service is a method of communication and data exchange between different software applications or systems involved in the eProcurement process. It enables interoperability and integration between various eProcurement platforms, systems, or other software solutions. By using standard web protocols, such as HTTP, it allows different components of an eProcurement ecosystem to interact and share information seamlessly. This facilitates efficient and streamlined procurement processes, enabling organizations to connect and exchange data with suppliers, manage procurement workflows, and optimize their procurement activities.
